sourceMovie.title: Blindness
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Blindness works as a drama movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The drama and mystery blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Maze Runner
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Maze Runner (2014) is the natural next step after Blindness: same mystery DNA, different perspective. The tense streak and science-fiction elevate it well above a simple genre exercise.

recommendations.title: I Am Legend
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Fans of Blindness who respond to its drama will find I Am Legend (2007) immediately familiar. Both films share a science-fiction sensibility without ever feeling like carbon copies of each other.

recommendations.title: Contagion
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: There's a reason Blindness fans keep landing on Contagion (2011) — the drama is unmistakably related even when the plots diverge. Both films operate on the same emotional frequency, especially in their third acts.

recommendations.title: Murder on the Orient Express
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Murder on the Orient Express draws from the same drama well as Blindness, channeling mystery in ways that feel unmistakably related. The 1974 film makes a natural companion watch — similar in spirit but distinctly its own.

recommendations.title: Soylent Green
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Soylent Green (1973) captures the mystery appeal of Blindness without simply imitating it. The science-fiction streak running through both films is what puts Soylent Green at the top of any "if you liked…" shortlist.

recommendations.title: The Omega Man
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The drama appeal of Blindness — that tense edge, the science-fiction — translates cleanly into The Omega Man (1971). Different setting, same essential satisfaction.

recommendations.title: World War Z
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The filmmakers behind World War Z (2013) clearly understood what makes science-fiction work — the same quality that keeps Blindness rewatchable. Expect the same dark rhythm, executed with a fresh angle.

recommendations.title: Maze Runner: The Scorch Trials
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Maze Runner: The Scorch Trials (2015) is the kind of film Blindness fans discover and immediately send to their group chat. The science-fiction overlap is real, but it's the tense emotional pull that seals the recommendation.

recommendations.title: Maze Runner: The Death Cure
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Maze Runner: The Death Cure shares Blindness's gift for science-fiction — the 2018 film delivers thriller without overstaying its welcome. Consider it required viewing once you've finished rewatching your first pick.

recommendations.title: The Book of Eli
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Much of what makes Blindness compelling — the science-fiction, the thriller — shows up in The Book of Eli (2010) in a slightly different form. Both films understand that tense storytelling works best when it trusts the audience.

faq.question: What should I watch after Blindness?
faq.answer: Try The Maze Runner, I Am Legend, Contagion—each shares DNA with Blindness while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Blindness?
faq.answer: Blindness (2008) is best described as Drama, Mystery, Science Fiction, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Blindness worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.5/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
